Church and state
- Ecclesiastical power superior to civil:
b Appoints kings
1Sa 10:1
b Directs administration
1Sa 15:4
b Reproves rulers
1Sa 15:35
b Withdraws support and anoints a successor
1Sa 16:13; 2Ki 9:26; 2Ki 11:12
b Attempted usurpation of ecclesiastical functions by civil authorities, reproved
1Sa 13:14; 2Ch 26:21
- State superior to the church:
b David, in organizing the priests and Levites in courses, and appointing musicians, instruments, and other details of religious services
1Ch 23; 2Ch 35:4
b Solomon, in thrusting Abiathar out of the high priest's office
1Ki 2:27
b Solomon, in over-shadowing the ecclesiastical in building the temple, and officiating primarily in the dedication, intercessory or priestly prayer, pronouncing the benediction, etc.
1Ki 5
b Hezekiah, in reorganizing temple service
2Ch 31:19
b Jeroboam, in subverting the Jewish religion
1Ki 12:33
b Manasseh, in subverting, and afterward restoring, the true religion
2Ch 33:9; 2Ch 33:17
b Jehoash, in supervising the repairs of the temple
2Ki 12:18
b Ahaz, in transforming the altars
2Ki 16:16
b Josiah, in exercising the function of the priests in the temple
2Ch 34:33
- State favorable to the church:
b Cyrus, in his proclamation to restore the temple at Jerusalem
2Ch 36:23; Ezr 1:11
b Darius, in his edict furthering the restoration of the temple
Ezr 6:14
b Artaxerxes, in exempting priests, Levites, and other temple functionaries from taxes
Ezr 7:24
